Title: A chromosome-level genome assembly for Ficus carica provides genetic insights into flowerless fig fruit development, psoralen biosynthesis, and drought tolerance

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activation of FcPP2C5 by FcJA2 is associated with enhanced drought tolerance, increased antioxidant enzyme activities, and improved ROS scavenging
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq correlation analysis; RT-qPCR in FcJA2-overexpressing plants; yeast one-hybrid; dual-luciferase assay; Arabidopsis protoplast LUC assay
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FcPP2C5 was among the drought-responsive genes most strongly correlated with FcJA2. 2. FcJA2 directly bound and activated the FcPP2C5 promoter. 3. FcPP2C5 is part of the FcJA2-mediated drought-response module that improves stress tolerance.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced under PEG6000 drought treatment; elevated in FcJA2-overexpressing plants
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful downstream marker/target in engineering drought-tolerant fig through FcJA2-centered regulatory module
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core experimentally validated downstream gene in the FcJA2 drought module, although not itself directly functionally transformed in this paper.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: osmotic adjustment, ROS mitigation, and drought tolerance
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activation of FcP5CS by FcJA2 promotes proline-associated osmotic regulation and contributes to improved drought tolerance
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq correlation analysis; RT-qPCR in FcJA2-overexpressing plants; yeast one-hybrid; dual-luciferase assay; Arabidopsis protoplast LUC assay
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FcP5CS was highly positively correlated with FcJA2 under drought. 2. FcJA2 directly bound and activated the FcP5CS promoter. 3. The FcJA2–FcP5CS module likely contributes to proline accumulation and drought tolerance.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced under PEG6000 drought treatment; significantly elevated in FcJA2-overexpressing plants
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate downstream target for engineering drought-tolerant fig via enhanced proline biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Function is inferred through direct promoter activation and physiological association rather than standalone transgenic validation of FcP5CS in this study.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate colored anthocyanidin precursors of anthocyanins
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: fruit pigmentation and human health benefits including prevention of heart disease and cancer
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression significantly increases anthocyanin accumulation and fruit red coloration
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Developmental transcriptomics; correlation with anthocyanin content; transient overexpression via pedicel transport; metabolite quantification
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step supported, especially in sarcocarp during S2
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly upregulated in sarcocarp at S2; peak expression at final fruit stage in Zibao; higher in mature/coloring fruits across Moraceae comparisons
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FcANS was one of the most strongly induced anthocyanin-pathway genes during sarcocarp anthocyanin accumulation. 2. Its expression increased by more than 30-fold at the critical S2 stage. 3. Transient overexpression increased anthocyanin content by 204.1% and deepened red fruit coloration.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for improving fig fruit color and anthocyanin nutritional quality, especially for sarcocarp pigmentation; valuable for gene stacking with CHS/UFGT
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter contains MYB, MYC, and W-box cis-elements; proposed to have conserved anthocyanin-related function across Moraceae.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Entry step to flavonoid pathway feeding anthocyanin branch
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies committed precursor pool for anthocyanin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: fruit pigmentation and human health benefits including prevention of heart disease and cancer
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression significantly increases anthocyanin accumulation and fruit red coloration, especially associated with pericarp pigmentation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Developmental transcriptomics; correlation analysis with anthocyanin content; transient overexpression via pedicel transport; metabolite quantification
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step supported, especially in pericarp during S3
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced in pericarp at S3; >6-fold increase at S3; peak expression at final fruit stage in Zibao; associated with mature/coloring fruits in Moraceae
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FcCHS10 had the strongest correlation with anthocyanin content among FcCHS genes in pericarp. 2. Its expression increased markedly during the S3 coloration stage. 3. Transient overexpression increased anthocyanin content by 230.4% and intensified red coloration.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for enhancing peel color and anthocyanin nutritional quality in fig and possibly other Moraceae fruits; suitable for stacking with FcANS
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter contains MYB, MYC, and W-box cis-elements; distinct tissue bias suggests stronger role in pericarp than sarcocarp.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Assigned as a key enzyme in the furanocoumarin pathway required for psoralen biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for biosynthesis of the target product psoralen
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: pharmacological activities against osteoporosis, tumors, viral and bacterial infections, and inflammation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: roots primarily; also present in stems, leaves, fruits, and latex
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increases psoralen accumulation, whereas silencing reduces psoralen accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Integrated transcriptome-metabolome correlation; Agrobacterium rhizogenes-mediated overexpression; VIGS silencing; psoralen quantification
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in roots relative to other organs
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FcMS expression was strongly positively correlated with psoralen accumulation, with r = 0.997 for psoralen. 2. Overexpression of FC11G00178 increased psoralen content by 85%. 3. Silencing of FC11G00178 decreased psoralen content by 47%, confirming it as critical for psoralen biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for engineering or selecting fig germplasm with elevated psoralen production, especially in roots; useful for medicinal metabolite production systems
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A closely related homolog, FC11G00180, despite 93% sequence similarity and strong overexpression, did not increase psoralen, supporting functional specificity of FC11G00178; described as unique to Ficus among surveyed Moraceae.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NAC transcription factor with conserved NAM domain
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Drought-response metabolism including osmotic regulation and ROS-scavenging capacity
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: osmotic adjustment and protection against drought-induced oxidative stress
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression significantly improves drought resistance, increasing proline and antioxidant enzyme activities while lowering H2O2 and MDA
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Direct transcriptional activation and positive autoregulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates FcPP2C5, FcP5CS, and its own promoter
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Central drought regulator linking stress signaling to proline accumulation and ROS detoxification
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: PEG6000 drought transcriptomics; RT-qPCR; subcellular localization; overexpression in F. carica; VIGS silencing; yeast one-hybrid; dual-luciferase assay; Arabidopsis protoplast LUC assay
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Significantly upregulated at all drought time points
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: PEG6000-induced drought stress; drought and rewatering assays
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Positive autoregulation via binding and activating its own promoter
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FcJA2 was the NAC family member most strongly correlated with drought-responsive genes. 2. Overexpression enhanced drought tolerance, antioxidant capacity, and proline accumulation, while silencing caused drought sensitivity. 3. FcJA2 directly activates FcPP2C5 and FcP5CS and positively regulates its own promoter.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Excellent candidate for developing drought-tolerant fig and related Moraceae crops by transgenic overexpression or promoter engineering, potentially coupled with downstream module markers FcPP2C5 and FcP5CS
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper states that FcJA2-mediated drought-response modules are conserved across Moraceae.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: High pericarp and fruit-bud expression is associated with syconium formation and enclosed receptacle/perianth development
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Acts with A/E-function MADS genes to promote receptacle and perianth developmental program in fig
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Considered a critical regulator of the distinctive syconium architecture
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative transcriptomics across tissues, stages, and species/cultivars; phylogenetic analysis; synteny; Ka/Ks selection analysis
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in pericarp and fruit buds, sustained through development
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Likely acts within MADS-box developmental complexes, but direct complex membership not experimentally tested here
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FcAGL6 showed elevated expression in pericarp and fruit buds of F. carica compared with related Moraceae. 2. It is under purifying selection and syntenically conserved. 3. The study proposes FcAGL6 as a critical regulator of syconium development.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for developmental engineering of fig fruit architecture, though direct trait deployment remains preliminary
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it is emphasized as a core conserved MADS-box regulator in the abstract and results, though direct transgenic functional perturbation was not performed.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Sustained high expression in pericarp and fruit buds is associated with development of the fig syconium
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Acts with A/E-function genes in perianth and receptacle development
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Highlighted as a critical regulator of fig receptacle development and syconium architecture
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative transcriptomics across tissues, stages, cultivars, and species; synteny; Ka/Ks selection analysis
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in fruit bud and pericarp across development
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FcAP2 was markedly upregulated in F. carica fruit tissues relative to Morus and Broussonetia. 2. It remained highly expressed through development in pericarp. 3. The study identifies it as a critical regulator of receptacle development in the syconium.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for altering fig fruit architecture and developmental morphology
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Developmental regulator rather than nutrient-metabolism gene; included because the paper treats it as a core gene module.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: High and sustained expression in fruit buds/pericarp is associated with syconium formation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Acts with A/E-function developmental genes in receptacle/perianth program
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Identified as a critical regulator of fig receptacle development and syconium architecture
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative transcriptomics across tissues, stages, cultivars, and species; synteny; Ka/Ks selection analysis
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in fruit buds and pericarp across development
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Likely MADS-box complex involvement, not directly validated
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FcSEP1/2 remained highly expressed in fig fruit buds and pericarp. 2. Comparative analyses support evolutionary conservation and purifying selection. 3. The study proposes FcSEP1/2 as critical regulators of syconium development.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential developmental engineering target for fruit architecture in fig
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Development-focused rather than nutrient-metabolism focused; retained because emphasized among core trait genes in this paper.
